summary: See clues file for full details. 2 tossups, 8 bonuses found.

works.name: General / Key Works
works.description: Analysis from 2 tossups and 8 bonuses. See clues file for detailed breakdown.

clues.clue: This author wrote a text whose second book opens by retelling the story of Narcissus gazing at his own reflection to compare his own profession with "embracing" the "surface of the pool." Anthony Grafton's book about this author discusses his tendency to "personalize" his "ancient sources" in his written works, such as one in which this author cites the story of a cobbler antagonizing Alexander the Great's personal portraitist.
examples: This author wrote a text whose second book opens by retelling the story of Narcissus gazing at his own reflection to compare his own profession with "embracing" the "surface of the pool." Anthony Grafton's book about this author discusses his tendency to "personalize" his "ancient sources" in his written works, such as one in which this author cites the story of a cobbler antagonizing Alexander the Great's personal portraitist.

clues.clue: This man suggested that artists populate their works with a "variety of things" so that the "soul of the viewer" will be moved through a narrative mode called historia.
examples: This man suggested that artists populate their works with a "variety of things" so that the "soul of the viewer" will be moved through a narrative mode called historia.

clues.clue: He was inspired by Vitruvius to write De Re Aedificatoria, a ten-book treatise on architecture.
examples: He was inspired by Vitruvius to write De Re Aedificatoria, a ten-book treatise on architecture.

clues.clue: This architect designed the facade for the Santa Maria Novella, and he wrote a 1435 book that was the first to suggest that artists use a vanishing point to create illusionistic perspective.
examples: This architect designed the facade for the Santa Maria Novella, and he wrote a 1435 book that was the first to suggest that artists use a vanishing point to create illusionistic perspective.

clues.clue: This architect designed the church of San Siro in Milan.
examples: This architect designed the church of San Siro in Milan.

clues.clue: Another work by this architect was commissioned by the Rucellai family.
examples: Another work by this architect was commissioned by the Rucellai family.

clues.clue: That work by this architect has niches flanked by flat pilasters on the lower level and an oriel window with paired pilasters.
examples: That work by this architect has niches flanked by flat pilasters on the lower level and an oriel window with paired pilasters.

clues.clue: That facade also notably has scrolls linking the upper and lower register and, is part of Santa Maria Novella.
examples: That facade also notably has scrolls linking the upper and lower register and, is part of Santa Maria Novella.

clues.clue: A church this man designed has a deep-arched entrance inside a temple front beneath another arch.
examples: A church this man designed has a deep-arched entrance inside a temple front beneath another arch.

clues.clue: This man's most famous building held a chapel of the planets and its Gothic sides were covered with arches holding (*) sarcophagi.
examples: This man's most famous building held a chapel of the planets and its Gothic sides were covered with arches holding (*) sarcophagi.

clues.clue: That building, planned to have a large dome at the crossing, was proclaimed "full of pagan gods and profane things" and its entrance contains three equal arches, two of which were designed to hold the sarcophagi of its patron and his wife Ixotta.
examples: That building, planned to have a large dome at the crossing, was proclaimed "full of pagan gods and profane things" and its entrance contains three equal arches, two of which were designed to hold the sarcophagi of its patron and his wife Ixotta.
